pi_items.verified_value: Data file exists at /workspace/data/4StoreyBuilding.inp but mounted path mapping differs from stated project_path
pi_items.source: File system inspection and setup_equest.ps1 script analysis

pi_items.verified_value: 0.35 is a reasonable target shading coefficient (SC) for high-performance Low-E windows, falling within typical Low-E window SC range of 0.10-0.40
pi_items.source: Web search on Low-E window specifications and SC values; established conversion SC = SHGC / 0.87

pi_items.verified_value: 0.28 corresponds to U-value of approximately 0.265, which falls within high-performance Low-E window range of 0.15-0.30
pi_items.source: Glass conductance to U-value calculation: U = 1 / (0.2 + 1/Conductance); verifier accepts this value
Verifier implementation uses this tolerance for SC and conductance comparisons (lines 97-98)

pi_items.verified_value: Model contains three glass types: 'Window Type #1 GT' (code 2004), 'Window Type #2 GT' (code 2203), and 'Door Type #1 GT' (code 1001). These use default SC and conductance values from GLASS-TYPE-CODE library.
pi_items.source: Grep analysis of 4StoreyBuilding.inp file; no explicit SHADING-COEF or GLASS-CONDUCTANCE parameters set
install_equest.ps1 script specifies exact download URL and build number

pi_items.verified_value: The metadata field 'target_shgc' is actually used as a shading coefficient (SC) value, not as SHGC. According to industry standards, SC = SHGC / 0.87. The field should be labeled 'target_sc' for clarity.
pi_items.source: Verifier.py line 59: 'target_sc = metadata.get("target_shgc", 0.35)'; comparison with SC = SHGC / 0.87 relationship

privileged_info_summary: The 4StoreyBuilding is a synthetic building model with three glass types (Window Type #1, Window Type #2, Door Type #1) using default GLASS-TYPE-CODE values. The task targets a parametric window retrofit reducing glass properties to SHADING-COEF = 0.35 and GLASS-CONDUCTANCE = 0.28 (equivalent to U-value ~0.265). These target values are reasonable for high-performance Low-E windows. The 4StoreyBuilding.inp data file exists in the environment. eQUEST 3.65 Build 7175 (2018-10-04) is the specified version. A naming inconsistency exists: the metadata field 'target_shgc' is actually treated as a shading coefficient (SC) value, not as SHGC (Solar Heat Gain Coefficient).
